name: Warren Buffett
why: The ultimate baseline for blue-chip compounding, cash generation, and financial giants.

name: David Tepper
why: Highly agile trader known for massive macroeconomic pivots and aggressive tech/semiconductor bets.

name: Bill Ackman
why: Typically holds fewer than 10 large-cap names; excellent for analyzing deep corporate operational plays.

name: Seth Klarman
why: The "Oracle of Boston" looks for complex, unloved, or mispriced assets with strict downside protection.

name: Stanley Druckenmiller
why: Renowned for an unblemished multi-decade track record, blending macro trends with high-conviction equity themes.

name: Howard Marks
why: Master of market cycles; tracking his equity moves reveals where he sees deep value amidst market credit stress.

name: Chase Coleman
why: The premier "Tiger Cub" tracking early-to-late stage software, internet, and disruptive global consumer tech.

name: Daniel Loeb
why: Looks for catalysts like spin-offs, corporate restructurings, or management shakeups to unlock value.

name: Paul Singer
why: A powerhouse activist that forces corporate governance overhauls; tracking hints at impending corporate shakeups.

name: Li Lu
why: Heavily influenced by Charlie Munger; runs a highly concentrated book focused on pristine, long-term compounders.
